36	# Lean Auditor
37	
38	You read **every `.lean` file in the project** and produce a per-file checklist of outdated comments, suspect definitions, dead-end proofs, and bad Lean practices. You are **read-only** and your only writable target is your own report under `task_results/`.
39	
40	You are **highly recommended in the review phase**: the review agent dispatches you whenever any `.lean` file has been modified this iter, OR whenever the prior verdict had live must-fix findings. Your output flags issues the prover and plan agents may have missed.
41	
42	## Stance: no strategic bias
43	
44	You audit the Lean **as Lean**. Your directive will NOT include the project strategy, the blueprint, or "what we are trying to prove" — and that is intentional. The point is to detect mathematically-wrong definitions and dead-end proofs *without* being swayed by what the project assumes should be true. A definition is wrong if it is wrong by reading the Lean alone (e.g. its body contradicts standard mathematical usage of the named concept); you do not need to know the strategy to spot that.
45	
46	This stance is also what makes you **critical of excuse-comments**. Lean-side comments like:
47	
48	- `-- We decided to use a temporary wrong definition; will fix it later.`
49	- `-- This proof is incomplete but works for now.`
50	- `-- TODO: replace before merging.`
51	- `-- Stand-in definition until we figure out the right one.`
52	
53	are RED FLAGS, not project workflow. Authors writing such comments are admitting the code is wrong; the comments cannot be allowed to silence the alarm. Flag every such comment with severity **major** at minimum — and **critical** when the named declaration is load-bearing.
54	
55	## Directive Format
56	
57	```markdown
58	# Lean Auditor Directive
59	
60	## Slug
61	<slug>
62	
63	## Scope (files)
64	<"all" by default. Optionally a list of file paths if the review agent wants to narrow scope; never expand beyond the project.>
65	
66	## Focus areas (optional)
67	<directories or namespaces to pay extra attention to. Bias toward thoroughness — do not skip the rest of the project just because focus is named.>
68	
69	## Known issues
70	<things the review agent already knows and doesn't want re-reported>
71	```
72	
73	The directive **does not** include STRATEGY.md, PROGRESS.md, references, or a description of what the project is trying to prove. That is by design.
74	
75	## What you do
76	
77	1. Read your directive.
78	2. **List every `.lean` file** under the project. Use `find` or `ls -R` — do not rely on import graphs (some files may be temporarily orphaned).
79	3. **For each file** (every one, no scope shortcuts):
80	   - Read the whole file.
81	   - For each declaration: is the signature reasonable as Lean? Is the body suspect (uses `axiom`, `Classical.choice _`, `sorry` with a body that looks like fake content, `:= True`, `:= rfl` on a non-trivial claim)?
82	   - For each comment: is it stale (referencing removed code or old strategies)? Is it an *excuse-comment* (see above)? Is it accurate about the code below it?
83	   - For each proof: is the tactic chain reasonable for the goal type, or does it look like it is going in the wrong direction (e.g. unfolding to manipulate syntax instead of using the available structure lemmas)?
84	   - Note any bad Lean practices: misuse of universe polymorphism, redundant typeclasses, deprecated tactics, anti-patterns like proving `False`-style lemmas to bypass infrastructure.
85	4. **Produce a per-file checklist** + a flagged-issues block grouped by severity.
86	
87	You may use:
88	- `archon-lean-lsp` MCP tools (read-only): hover, signature lookup, diagnostics. Useful to verify a declaration's actual type.
89	- `sorry_analyzer` if available, to get sorry counts per file.
90	- Standard `Read` / `Grep` for code reading.
91	
92	You may NOT modify any project file. Even when a fix is obvious.
93	
94	## Report format
95	
96	Write your report to `.archon/task_results/lean-auditor-<slug>.md` (or the parent-aware path under `task_results/<parent-slug>/` when invoked nested — your invocation prompt names the exact path).
97

100

102	<slug>
103	
104	## Iteration
105	<NNN>
106	
107	## Scope
108	- files audited: <N>
109	- files skipped (per directive): <N> — <reason>
110	
111	## Per-file checklist
112	
113	### <path/to/file.lean>
114	- **outdated comments**: <N flagged> (or "none")
115	- **suspect definitions**: <N flagged> (or "none")
116	- **dead-end proofs**: <N flagged> (or "none")
117	- **bad practices**: <N flagged> (or "none")
118	- **excuse-comments**: <N flagged> (or "none")
119	- **notes**:
120	  - <one bullet per finding, naming line number where relevant>
121	  - ...
122	
123	### <next file>
124	- ...
125	
126	(One block per file. Cover every file, including the ones with no findings — they get `none` everywhere and `notes: -`.)
127	
128	## Must-fix-this-iter
129	
130	Apply verbatim. Every one of the following lands here automatically, no exceptions, no under-classification:
131	
132	- **Excuse-comments** on any declaration: `-- TODO replace with real def`, `-- placeholder`, `-- temporary`, `-- wrong but works`, `-- will fix later`. These are admissions that the code is wrong; treat them as wrong.
133	- **Weakened-wrong definitions**: a `def Foo := <something structurally different>` standing in for the real concept (e.g. `Scheme.LineBundle := CommRing.Pic …` when the real definition is an invertible quasi-coherent sheaf).
134	- **Parallel APIs of existing Mathlib**: a project file that copy-and-modifies a Mathlib definition (changing one type-class or argument) instead of instancing the existing Mathlib version. Bridge lemmas needed to consume it are the symptom.
135	- **Suspect bodies on substantive claims**: `:= sorry` on a load-bearing claim, `:= True`, `:= rfl` on a non-trivial statement, `:= Classical.choice _` without authorization.
136	- **Axioms** on non-trivial claims that the project hasn't explicitly authorized in the strategy.
137	
138	A finding that meets any of the above lands at must-fix-this-iter — even if classifying it as "major" would feel more diplomatic. Soft severity is how wrong code hardens into the project.
139	
140	- `<file>:<line>` — <description>. Why must-fix: <one line>.
141	- ...
142	
143	## Major
144	
145	Findings that are real issues but don't meet the must-fix bar — typically stale comments that don't actively mislead, naming drift, minor code smells.
146	
147	- `<file>:<line>` — <description>.
148	- ...
149	
150	## Minor
151	
152	Low-impact observations.
153	
154	- `<file>:<line>` — <description>.
155	- ...
156	
157	## Excuse-comments (always called out separately)
158	
159	If any excuse-comments were flagged, list each one verbatim with its file:line. These deserve special visibility because they document the project lying to itself.
160	
161	- `Foo/Bar.lean:42`: "TODO: replace with the real definition" (attached to `Foo.bar`, which is a load-bearing definition in `Baz.lean`). Severity: critical.
162	- ...
163	
164	## Severity summary
165	
166	- **must-fix-this-iter**: <N> — these block downstream work in their files until addressed (see plan.md's per-file gate).
167	- **major**: <N>
168	- **minor**: <N>
169	- **excuse-comments**: <N> (also counted under must-fix-this-iter above; called out separately because they document the project lying to itself).
170	
171	Overall verdict: one sentence.
172	```
173	
174	**Strict severity reminder.** The plan agent's gate treats must-fix-this-iter as blocking. If you under-classify a wrong-decision finding to keep the loop's momentum, you are working against the project — wrong code accumulates faster than reviewers can catch it later. When in doubt, classify higher, not lower.
175	
176	## Return value
177	
178	Your final assistant message:
179	
180	- One line: `<slug>: <overall verdict> — <N> files audited, <M> issues (critical/major/minor: a/b/c)`
181	- The path to your full report.
182	
183	## Reminders
184	
185	- **Read every file. No scope shortcuts.**
186	- **No strategic context.** Your audit is unbiased by design.
187	- **You are read-only.** No project source, no blueprint, no state files (except your own report).
188	- **Excuse-comments are red flags.** Critical or major severity, every time.
189	- **Per-file checklist is the primary output.** Cover every file; the format is fixed.

1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are one of: the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (per descriptor in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	When instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**:
8	
9	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` override Archon's global prompts.
10	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` override globally installed plugins.
11	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project.
12	
13	## Skills
14	
15	- **archon-lean4** — installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source). Provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, etc.
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List with `ls .claude/tools/`; run `<path> --help`. MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs for an informal proof-style sketch when you're stuck on an approach and want a second opinion. Supports DeepSeek, Kimi (OpenAI-compatible via `--provider kimi`), Kimi (Anthropic-compatible via `--provider kimi-anthropic`), OpenRouter, OpenAI, and Gemini; defaults to `--provider auto` which picks the best available key automatically. **Requires at least one key in env** (`DEEPSEEK_API_KEY` / `MOONSHOT_API_KEY` / `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` / `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY`) — check via `env | grep -E "DEEPSEEK|MOONSHOT|OPENROUTER|OPENAI|GEMINI"` BEFORE planning around its use; if no key is set, fall back to alternatives below. Both `kimi` and `kimi-anthropic` use the same `MOONSHOT_API_KEY`; the latter speaks Moonshot's Anthropic-compatible Messages endpoint and supports `--think`. The output is LLM-generated from training data, NOT source-derived: this tool is **not** a literature retriever. For actual literature lookup (cross-check a claim against Hartshorne, fetch an arXiv paper, summarize a Stacks tag) consult the auto-injected subagent catalog — when a literature/reference-fetching subagent is enabled it will appear there — or use the `WebSearch` / `WebFetch` tools directly.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (one wrapper handles every subagent — see "Subagents" below).
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter — `name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`, optional `dispatcher_notes` — followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude reads).
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents.** The plan and review prompts auto-inject an **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation listing every enabled descriptor with description, write-domain, MANDATORY flag, and `dispatcher_notes`. When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
31	
32	**Invoke** via the generic wrapper (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Treat each dispatch as blocking — wait for its report before acting on it.** Don't *deliberately* set `run_in_background: true`. But note the wrapper is genuinely synchronous (it `subprocess.run`s the `archon subagent` CLI and only returns once the child finishes and its report is on disk), so the harness may *auto-background* a long-running dispatch and hand you a task ID immediately — that's expected and fine. When it does, await that task / poll for the report file at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md` (the wrapper's one-line status and exit 0 confirm completion) before you read or act on the result. The thing to avoid is firing a dispatch and then continuing to plan as if it had already returned; whether the call blocked inline or was auto-backgrounded, the rule is the same: do not consume a subagent's output until its report exists.
45	
46	**Highly-recommended subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` is treated as **highly recommended** for the plan phase (similarly `[review]` for the review phase). The catalog tags them `[HIGHLY RECOMMENDED]`. Dispatch each one unless you have a concrete reason to skip — when skipping, record a one-line rationale under a `## Subagent skips` section in `iter/iter-NNN/<phase>.md` (e.g. `- strategy-critic: STRATEGY.md SHA unchanged from prior iter and last verdict was SOUND`). A post-phase audit checks both signals: it silences when the subagent dispatched OR a skip rationale was recorded, and warns only when neither happened. Each subagent's `dispatcher_notes` enumerates its specific skip conditions — read them before deciding. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no recommended dispatch is required. (The frontmatter field is still named `mandatory: [...]` for backward compat with existing configs and descriptors — its semantic meaning is "highly recommended", not "must dispatch".)
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All Archon state files are in `.archon/`. The table below covers **phase agents** (plan, prover, review) and the **user**. Subagent permissions are NOT a fixed row — they are descriptor-driven (`write_domain` glob in the subagent's frontmatter, enforced at dispatch time). See "Subagent permissions" after the table.
51	
52	| File | Plan | Prover | Review | User |
53	|------|------|--------|--------|------|
54	| `PROGRESS.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
55	| `STRATEGY.md` | read + write | — | — | read |
56	| `USER_HINTS.md` | **loop-managed** (captured + cleared by the CLI; injected into the plan prompt) | — | — | write |
57	| `task_pending.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
58	| `task_done.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
59	| `task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect) | write (own file) | read only | read |
60	| `proof-journal/` | read | — | **write** | read |
61	| `PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | — | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log lives in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`) | read |
62	| `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | — | read (recent window injected) | read |
63	| `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (recent window injected) | — | **write** (this iter only) | read |
64	| `iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write | — | read | read |
65	| `archon-protected.yaml` | read | read | read | **write** |
66	| `.lean` files | — | write (own file; frozen protected signatures) | — | write (via comments) |
67	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints) | read | **write** (semantic markers: `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections — NOT `\leanok`) | read |
68	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | — | — | read |
69	| `TO_USER.md` | **write** (shared notice board) | **write** (shared notice board) | **write** (shared notice board) | read |
70	
71	### Subagent permissions
72	
73	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:
74	
75	- `write_domain: "<glob>"` — file pattern the subagent may write. Empty for purely read-only subagents (the dispatching call typically passes `--write-domain 'task_results/**'` so the subagent can still write its own report).
76	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.
77	- `can_spawn: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may dispatch children whose `write_domain` is a subset of its own.
78	
79	The dispatcher enforces `write_domain` at the filesystem level: any child whose declared domain is not a subset of the parent's is rejected before the child agent starts. Reads are restricted only by the descriptor's prompt body (e.g. fresh-context critics that are told not to read PROGRESS.md / STRATEGY.md).
80	
81	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`** is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow run by hand by the mathematician. The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. To invoke a refactor-style subagent inside the loop, write the directive to a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md` and dispatch via the generic wrapper. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise.
82	
83	## Protected Declarations
84	
85	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Rules:
86	
87	- **Plan / prover / review** agents: read-only on protected signatures. May fill proof bodies; may not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
88	- **Write-capable structural subagents** (per their `write_domain`): may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (name + signature preserved verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
89	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.
90	
91	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
92	
93	Two semantic markers:
94	
95	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
96	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
97	
98	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
99	
100	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves.
101	
102	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
103	
104	## User Interaction
105	
106	The user provides hints in two places:
107	
108	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
109	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
110	
111	Archon talks back to the user through **`TO_USER.md`** — a single, *persistent* notice board surfaced as a UI banner. It is **shared and writable by every agent** (plan, prover, review). It is no longer cleared at the start of each review phase; content survives across iters until an agent prunes it. The discipline every writer follows:
112	
113	- **Concise**: the whole file stays ≤ 2–3 short bullets. It is a banner, not a log.
114	- **Relevant now**: before adding anything, read the current content and **delete any bullet that is no longer true at this point** (the blocker was resolved, the decision was overtaken, the credential got set). Never let it accumulate stale notices.
115	- **Notice board, never a question queue**: the loop is autonomous and may run unattended for many iters. Never write "awaiting your decision", an options menu, a "where to reply", or "default to X if no reply" — anything implying the loop is paused for a human. State decisions as *made* and blockers as *worked-around-as-far-as-possible*; the user steers asynchronously via `USER_HINTS.md`.
116	
117	## Agent Roles
118	
119	### Plan Agent
120	
121	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for full instructions.
122	- User hints, blueprint-doctor findings, recent iter sidecars, the subagent catalog, and the references summary are all pre-injected into your invocation prompt. You do not "go read" those files.
123	- Read `task_results/` to collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`.
124	- Optionally invoke subagents (via the auto-injected catalog) before writing prover objectives. Subagent reports are auto-archived to `logs/iter-NNN/` by the Archon CLI.
125	- Write `PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round.
126	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch markers — `\leanok` is owned by the deterministic sync; `\mathlibok` / `% NOTE:` by the review agent.
127	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself.
128	
129	### Prover Agent
130

271	      change transitionPreMap d r I I hI hI (MvPolynomial.C n) = algebraMap _ _ (MvPolynomial.C n)
272	      rw [transitionPreMap, MvPolynomial.aeval_C,
273	        IsScalarTower.algebraMap_apply ℤ (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
274	          (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I I hI hI)), MvPolynomial.algebraMap_eq]
275	    · intro e
276	      change transitionPreMap d r I I hI hI (MvPolynomial.X e) = algebraMap _ _ (MvPolynomial.X e)
277	      rw [transitionPreMap, MvPolynomial.aeval_X, himg, Matrix.map_apply]
278	      congr 1
279	      rw [universalMatrix, dif_neg e.2.2]
280	  apply IsLocalization.ringHom_ext (Submonoid.powers (minorDet d r I I hI hI))
281	  simp only [transitionMap, IsLocalization.Away.lift_comp, RingHom.id_comp]
282	  exact hpre

284	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — triple-overlap rings and the cocycle
285	
286	The cocycle condition `θ_{I,K} = θ_{I,J} ∘ θ_{J,K}` cannot be stated as a naive
287	composition of the landed `transitionMap`s: the codomain of `transitionMap d r J K`
288	(`R^J[1/P^J_K]`) differs from the domain of `transitionMap d r I J` (`R^J[1/P^J_I]`).
289	The identity therefore lives over the *triple-overlap* rings obtained by inverting BOTH
290	relevant minors in each chart ring:
291	`S_K := R^K[1/(P^K_I P^K_J)]`, `S_J := R^J[1/(P^J_I P^J_K)]`, `S_I := R^I[1/(P^I_J P^I_K)]`.
292	We build the localised transition maps `Θ_{I,J} : S_J →+* S_I`, `Θ_{J,K} : S_K →+* S_J`,
293	`Θ_{I,K} : S_K →+* S_I` over these rings and verify the cocycle.
294	
295	Blueprint reference: `lem:gr_cocycle` (`blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_GrassmannianCells.tex`). -/
296	
297	/-- A ring homomorphism carries the nonsingular (Cramer) inverse to the nonsingular
298	inverse, provided the determinant is a unit. Project-local helper: Mathlib has no direct
299	`map`-`nonsingular inverse` compatibility lemma. -/
300	private lemma map_nonsing_inv {n : ℕ} {R S : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing S] (f : R →+* S)
301	    (A : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) R) (h : IsUnit A.det) :
302	    (A.map f)⁻¹ = A⁻¹.map f := by
303	  have hmul : (A.map f) * (A⁻¹.map f) = 1 := by
304	    rw [← Matrix.map_mul, Matrix.mul_nonsing_inv A h, Matrix.map_one f (map_zero f) (map_one f)]
305	  exact Matrix.inv_eq_right_inv hmul
306	
307	/-- Inclusion of the away-localisation at `x` into the away-localisation at `x * y`
308	(inverting the extra factor `y`). Project-local: the structure map of the triple overlap
309	relative to a double-localisation. -/
310	noncomputable def awayInclLeft {R : Type*} [CommRing R] (x y : R) :
311	    Localization.Away x →+* Localization.Away (x * y) :=
312	  IsLocalization.Away.lift (S := Localization.Away x) x
313	    (g := algebraMap R (Localization.Away (x * y)))
314	    (by
315	      have h : IsUnit (algebraMap R (Localization.Away (x * y)) (x * y)) :=
316	        IsLocalization.Away.algebraMap_isUnit _
317	      rw [map_mul] at h
318	      exact isUnit_of_mul_isUnit_left h)
319	
320	/-- Inclusion of the away-localisation at `y` into the away-localisation at `x * y`
321	(inverting the extra factor `x`). Project-local. -/
322	noncomputable def awayInclRight {R : Type*} [CommRing R] (x y : R) :
323	    Localization.Away y →+* Localization.Away (x * y) :=
324	  IsLocalization.Away.lift (S := Localization.Away y) y
325	    (g := algebraMap R (Localization.Away (x * y)))
326	    (by
327	      have h : IsUnit (algebraMap R (Localization.Away (x * y)) (x * y)) :=
328	        IsLocalization.Away.algebraMap_isUnit _
329	      rw [map_mul] at h
330	      exact isUnit_of_mul_isUnit_right h)
331	
332	/-- `awayInclLeft` is the canonical map over `R`: it intertwines the two structure maps.
333	Project-local. -/
334	lemma awayInclLeft_comp_algebraMap {R : Type*} [CommRing R] (x y : R) :
335	    (awayInclLeft x y).comp (algebraMap R (Localization.Away x)) =
336	      algebraMap R (Localization.Away (x * y)) := by
337	  rw [awayInclLeft]; exact IsLocalization.Away.lift_comp x _
338	
339	/-- `awayInclRight` is the canonical map over `R`: it intertwines the two structure maps.
340	Project-local. -/
341	lemma awayInclRight_comp_algebraMap {R : Type*} [CommRing R] (x y : R) :
342	    (awayInclRight x y).comp (algebraMap R (Localization.Away y)) =
343	      algebraMap R (Localization.Away (x * y)) := by
344	  rw [awayInclRight]; exact IsLocalization.Away.lift_comp y _
345	
346	/-- The image of a general minor determinant `P^B_C` under the pre-localisation hom
347	`θ̃_{A,B}` is the determinant of the `C`-minor of the image matrix `M = (X^A_B)⁻¹ X^A`
348	(generalises the `C = A` computation underlying `lem:gr_transition_pre_unit`). Project-local. -/
349	theorem transitionPreMap_minorDet (d r : ℕ) (I J K : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
350	    (hJ : J.card = d) (hK : K.card = d) :
351	    transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ (minorDet d r J K hJ hK)
352	      = ((imageMatrix d r I J hI hJ).submatrix id
353	          (fun j : Fin d => (K.orderIsoOfFin hK j : Fin r))).det := by
354	  have e1 : transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ (minorDet d r J K hJ hK)
355	      = (((universalMatrix d r J hJ).submatrix id
356	          (fun j : Fin d => (K.orderIsoOfFin hK j : Fin r))).map
357	            (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ)).det :=
358	    RingHom.map_det (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ).toRingHom _
359	  rw [e1, ← Matrix.submatrix_map, universalMatrix_map_transitionPreMap]
360	
361	/-- The "cross" minor `P^B_C` is sent by `θ̃_{A,B}`, then pushed into a double localisation
362	`D` (in which `P^A_C` is a unit), to a unit. Concretely
363	`θ̃_{A,B}(P^B_C) = det((X^A_B)⁻¹ X^A_C) = det((X^A_B)⁻¹) · P^A_C`, a product of two units
364	once `P^A_C` is inverted. This is the cross-factor input to each triple-overlap transition
365	lift. Project-local. -/
366	private lemma isUnit_incl_transitionPreMap_cross
367	    (d r : ℕ) (A B C : Finset (Fin r)) (hA : A.card = d) (hB : B.card = d) (hC : C.card = d)
368	    {D : Type*} [CommRing D] [Algebra (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ A}) ℤ) D]
369	    (incl : Localization.Away (minorDet d r A B hA hB) →+* D)
370	    (hincl : incl.comp (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ A}) ℤ)
371	        (Localization.Away (minorDet d r A B hA hB)))
372	        = algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ A}) ℤ) D)
373	    (hunit : IsUnit (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ A}) ℤ) D
374	        (minorDet d r A C hA hC))) :
375	    IsUnit (incl (transitionPreMap d r A B hA hB (minorDet d r B C hB hC))) := by
376	  have hsub : (imageMatrix d r A B hA hB).submatrix id
377	        (fun j : Fin d => (C.orderIsoOfFin hC j : Fin r))
378	      = universalMinorInv d r A B hA hB *
379	        (((universalMatrix d r A hA).map (algebraMap _ _)).submatrix id
380	          (fun j : Fin d => (C.orderIsoOfFin hC j : Fin r))) := mul_submatrix_col _ _ _
381	  rw [transitionPreMap_minorDet, hsub, Matrix.det_mul, map_mul]
382	  refine IsUnit.mul ?_ ?_
383	  · refine IsUnit.map incl ?_
384	    refine IsUnit.of_mul_eq_one (universalMinor d r A B hA hB).det ?_
385	    rw [← Matrix.det_mul, (universalMinorInv_mul_cancel d r A B hA hB).1, Matrix.det_one]
386	  · have hdet : (((universalMatrix d r A hA).map
387	            (algebraMap _ (Localization.Away (minorDet d r A B hA hB)))).submatrix id
388	            (fun j : Fin d => (C.orderIsoOfFin hC j : Fin r))).det
389	          = algebraMap _ (Localization.Away (minorDet d r A B hA hB)) (minorDet d r A C hA hC) := by
390	      rw [Matrix.submatrix_map]
391	      exact (RingHom.map_det _ _).symm
392	    rw [hdet]
393	    have hcomp : incl (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ A}) ℤ)
394	        (Localization.Away (minorDet d r A B hA hB)) (minorDet d r A C hA hC))
395	        = algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ A}) ℤ) D (minorDet d r A C hA hC) :=
396	      RingHom.congr_fun hincl _
397	    rw [hcomp]
398	    exact hunit

400	/-- The left factor of a product is a unit in the away-localisation at that product.
401	Project-local. -/
402	private lemma isUnit_algebraMap_away_left {R : Type*} [CommRing R] (x y : R) :
403	    IsUnit (algebraMap R (Localization.Away (x * y)) x) := by
404	  have h : IsUnit (algebraMap R (Localization.Away (x * y)) (x * y)) :=
405	    IsLocalization.Away.algebraMap_isUnit _
406	  rw [map_mul] at h
407	  exact isUnit_of_mul_isUnit_left h
408	
409	/-- The right factor of a product is a unit in the away-localisation at that product.
410	Project-local. -/
411	private lemma isUnit_algebraMap_away_right {R : Type*} [CommRing R] (x y : R) :
412	    IsUnit (algebraMap R (Localization.Away (x * y)) y) := by
413	  have h : IsUnit (algebraMap R (Localization.Away (x * y)) (x * y)) :=
414	    IsLocalization.Away.algebraMap_isUnit _
415	  rw [map_mul] at h
416	  exact isUnit_of_mul_isUnit_right h

418	/-- The triple-overlap transition map `Θ_{I,J} : S_J →+* S_I`, where
419	`S_J = R^J[1/(P^J_I P^J_K)]` and `S_I = R^I[1/(P^I_J P^I_K)]`: the away-localisation lift of
420	`θ̃_{I,J}` (post-composed into `S_I`) along the doubly-inverted minor `P^J_I P^J_K`.
421	Project-local. -/
422	noncomputable def cocycleΘIJ (d r : ℕ) (I J K : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
423	    (hJ : J.card = d) (hK : K.card = d) :
424	    Localization.Away (minorDet d r J I hJ hI * minorDet d r J K hJ hK) →+*
425	      Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ * minorDet d r I K hI hK) :=
426	  IsLocalization.Away.lift (minorDet d r J I hJ hI * minorDet d r J K hJ hK)
427	    (g := (awayInclLeft (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK)).comp
428	            (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ).toRingHom)
429	    (by
430	      rw [map_mul]
431	      refine IsUnit.mul ?_ ?_
432	      · exact (isUnit_transitionPreMap_minorDet d r I J hI hJ).map
433	          (awayInclLeft (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK))
434	      · exact isUnit_incl_transitionPreMap_cross d r I J K hI hJ hK
435	          (awayInclLeft (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK))
436	          (awayInclLeft_comp_algebraMap _ _)
437	          (isUnit_algebraMap_away_right _ _))
438	
439	/-- The triple-overlap transition map `Θ_{J,K} : S_K →+* S_J`, where
440	`S_K = R^K[1/(P^K_I P^K_J)]` and `S_J = R^J[1/(P^J_I P^J_K)]`. Project-local. -/
441	noncomputable def cocycleΘJK (d r : ℕ) (I J K : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
442	    (hJ : J.card = d) (hK : K.card = d) :
443	    Localization.Away (minorDet d r K I hK hI * minorDet d r K J hK hJ) →+*
444	      Localization.Away (minorDet d r J I hJ hI * minorDet d r J K hJ hK) :=
445	  IsLocalization.Away.lift (minorDet d r K I hK hI * minorDet d r K J hK hJ)
446	    (g := (awayInclRight (minorDet d r J I hJ hI) (minorDet d r J K hJ hK)).comp
447	            (transitionPreMap d r J K hJ hK).toRingHom)
448	    (by
449	      rw [map_mul]
450	      refine IsUnit.mul ?_ ?_
451	      · exact isUnit_incl_transitionPreMap_cross d r J K I hJ hK hI
452	          (awayInclRight (minorDet d r J I hJ hI) (minorDet d r J K hJ hK))
453	          (awayInclRight_comp_algebraMap _ _)
454	          (isUnit_algebraMap_away_left _ _)
455	      · exact (isUnit_transitionPreMap_minorDet d r J K hJ hK).map
456	          (awayInclRight (minorDet d r J I hJ hI) (minorDet d r J K hJ hK)))
457	
458	/-- The triple-overlap transition map `Θ_{I,K} : S_K →+* S_I`, where
459	`S_K = R^K[1/(P^K_I P^K_J)]` and `S_I = R^I[1/(P^I_J P^I_K)]`. Project-local. -/
460	noncomputable def cocycleΘIK (d r : ℕ) (I J K : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
461	    (hJ : J.card = d) (hK : K.card = d) :
462	    Localization.Away (minorDet d r K I hK hI * minorDet d r K J hK hJ) →+*
463	      Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ * minorDet d r I K hI hK) :=
464	  IsLocalization.Away.lift (minorDet d r K I hK hI * minorDet d r K J hK hJ)
465	    (g := (awayInclRight (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK)).comp
466	            (transitionPreMap d r I K hI hK).toRingHom)
467	    (by
468	      rw [map_mul]
469	      refine IsUnit.mul ?_ ?_
470	      · exact (isUnit_transitionPreMap_minorDet d r I K hI hK).map
471	          (awayInclRight (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK))
472	      · exact isUnit_incl_transitionPreMap_cross d r I K J hI hK hJ
473	          (awayInclRight (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK))
474	          (awayInclRight_comp_algebraMap _ _)
475	          (isUnit_algebraMap_away_left _ _))

477	/-- Mapping a base-changed matrix through a further ring hom collapses to a single base change
478	when the homs compose correctly. Project-local helper. -/
479	private lemma map_map_eq_of_comp {m n : ℕ} {R A D : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing A] [CommRing D]
480	    (M : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin n) R) (f : R →+* A) (g : A →+* D) (h : R →+* D)
481	    (hcomp : g.comp f = h) : (M.map f).map g = M.map h := by
482	  rw [Matrix.map_map, ← RingHom.coe_comp, hcomp]
483	
484	/-- Mapping the image matrix `M = (X^I_X)⁻¹ X^I` through any ring hom `incl` lying over the
485	structure map `R^I → D` (i.e. `incl ∘ (R^I → R^I_X) = (R^I → D)`) yields `(Y_X)⁻¹ Y`, where
486	`Y := X^I` base-changed to `D`. The key reusable step in the cocycle computation. Project-local. -/
487	private lemma imageMatrix_map_eq (d r : ℕ) (I X : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
488	    (hX : X.card = d) {D : Type*} [CommRing D]
489	    [Algebra (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ) D]
490	    (incl : Localization.Away (minorDet d r I X hI hX) →+* D)
491	    (hincl : incl.comp (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
492	        (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I X hI hX)))
493	        = algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ) D) :
494	    (imageMatrix d r I X hI hX).map incl
495	      = (((universalMatrix d r I hI).map
496	            (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ) D)).submatrix id
497	          (fun j : Fin d => (X.orderIsoOfFin hX j : Fin r)))⁻¹ *
498	        (universalMatrix d r I hI).map
499	          (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ) D) := by
500	  have hmm : (imageMatrix d r I X hI hX).map incl
501	      = (universalMinorInv d r I X hI hX).map incl
502	        * ((universalMatrix d r I hI).map
503	            (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
504	              (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I X hI hX)))).map incl := by
505	    rw [imageMatrix]; exact Matrix.map_mul
506	  rw [hmm, map_map_eq_of_comp _ _ _ _ hincl, universalMinorInv,
507	    ← map_nonsing_inv incl (universalMinor d r I X hI hX)
508	        (isUnit_det_universalMinor d r I X hI hX)]
509	  congr 1
510	  rw [universalMinor, map_map_eq_of_comp _ _ _ _ hincl, ← Matrix.submatrix_map]
511	
512	/-- Matrix cancellation `(B⁻¹ A)(A⁻¹ M) = B⁻¹ M` when `A` is invertible. Project-local helper
513	for the final step of the cocycle. -/
514	private lemma inv_mul_inv_mul_cancel {d e : ℕ} {R : Type*} [CommRing R]
515	    (A B : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) R) (M : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin e) R) (hA : IsUnit A.det) :
516	    (B⁻¹ * A) * (A⁻¹ * M) = B⁻¹ * M := by
517	  rw [Matrix.mul_assoc B⁻¹ A (A⁻¹ * M), ← Matrix.mul_assoc A A⁻¹ M,
518	    Matrix.mul_nonsing_inv A hA, Matrix.one_mul]
519	
520	/-- The central matrix identity behind the cocycle condition: over the triple-overlap ring
521	`S_I`, the image matrix `(X^I_K)⁻¹ X^I` of `θ_{I,K}` equals `θ_{I,J}` applied entrywise to the
522	image matrix `(X^J_K)⁻¹ X^J` of `θ_{J,K}`. Both reduce to `(Y_K)⁻¹ Y` with `Y = X^I` over
523	`S_I`. Project-local. -/
524	private lemma cocycle_imageMatrix_eq (d r : ℕ) (I J K : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
525	    (hJ : J.card = d) (hK : K.card = d) :
526	    (imageMatrix d r I K hI hK).map
527	        (awayInclRight (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK))
528	      = (imageMatrix d r J K hJ hK).map
529	          ((cocycleΘIJ d r I J K hI hJ hK).comp
530	            (awayInclRight (minorDet d r J I hJ hI) (minorDet d r J K hJ hK))) := by
531	  -- LHS = (Y_K)⁻¹ * Y, where `Y := X^I` over `S_I`.
532	  have hLHS := imageMatrix_map_eq d r I K hI hK
533	    (awayInclRight (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK))
534	    (awayInclRight_comp_algebraMap _ _)
535	  -- `(imageMatrix I J).map (awayInclLeft …) = (Y_J)⁻¹ Y`.
536	  have hMJimg := imageMatrix_map_eq d r I J hI hJ
537	    (awayInclLeft (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK))
538	    (awayInclLeft_comp_algebraMap _ _)
539	  set Y := (universalMatrix d r I hI).map
540	      (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
541	        (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ * minorDet d r I K hI hK))) with hY
542	  -- Unit facts for the two minors of `Y`.
543	  have hYJ : IsUnit (Y.submatrix id (fun j : Fin d => (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ j : Fin r))).det := by
544	    have e : (Y.submatrix id (fun j : Fin d => (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ j : Fin r))).det
545	        = algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
546	            (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ * minorDet d r I K hI hK))
547	            (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) := by
548	      rw [hY, Matrix.submatrix_map]
549	      exact (RingHom.map_det _ _).symm
550	    rw [e]; exact isUnit_algebraMap_away_left _ _
551	  -- `M^J := θ_{I,J}(X^J) = (Y_J)⁻¹ Y` over `S_I`.
552	  have hχ : ((cocycleΘIJ d r I J K hI hJ hK).comp
553	        (awayInclRight (minorDet d r J I hJ hI) (minorDet d r J K hJ hK))).comp
554	          (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ J}) ℤ)
555	            (Localization.Away (minorDet d r J K hJ hK)))
556	      = (awayInclLeft (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK)).comp
557	          (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ).toRingHom := by
558	    rw [RingHom.comp_assoc, awayInclRight_comp_algebraMap, cocycleΘIJ]
559	    exact IsLocalization.Away.lift_comp _ _
560	  have hMJ : (universalMatrix d r J hJ).map
561	        ((awayInclLeft (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK)).comp
562	          (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ).toRingHom)
563	      = (Y.submatrix id (fun j : Fin d => (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ j : Fin r)))⁻¹ * Y := by
564	    have e1 : (universalMatrix d r J hJ).map
565	          ((awayInclLeft (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK)).comp
566	            (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ).toRingHom)
567	        = (imageMatrix d r I J hI hJ).map
568	            (awayInclLeft (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK)) := by
569	      rw [← map_map_eq_of_comp (universalMatrix d r J hJ)
570	          (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ).toRingHom
571	          (awayInclLeft (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK)) _ rfl]
572	      congr 1
573	      exact universalMatrix_map_transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ
574	    rw [e1, hMJimg]
575	  -- RHS = (M^J_K)⁻¹ M^J = (Y_K)⁻¹ Y.
576	  have hRHS : (imageMatrix d r J K hJ hK).map
577	        ((cocycleΘIJ d r I J K hI hJ hK).comp
578	          (awayInclRight (minorDet d r J I hJ hI) (minorDet d r J K hJ hK)))

21	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — scalar endomorphisms of the structure sheaf
22	
23	To realise a matrix of regular functions as a morphism of free sheaves of modules we
24	need to turn a global section `a ∈ Γ(X, ⊤)` of the structure sheaf into a scalar
25	endomorphism of `O_X` (= `SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf`). On the affine chart
26	`U^I = Spec R^I` the matrix entries of the universal matrix `X^I` live in `R^I`, and we
27	inject them into the global sections via `Scheme.ΓSpecIso`.
28	
29	These helpers are project-local: Mathlib has no ready-made "matrix ↦ morphism of free
30	sheaves" primitive. -/

32	variable {X : Scheme.{u}}

34	/-- The global section of the structure sheaf `O_X` (as a sheaf of modules over itself)
35	determined by a section `a ∈ Γ(X, ⊤)` over the whole space, by restriction to every open.
36	Project-local helper for building scalar endomorphisms. -/
37	noncomputable def globalUnitSection (a : Γ(X, ⊤)) :
38	    (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf).sections :=
39	  PresheafOfModules.sectionsMk
40	    (fun Y => (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op a : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj Y))
41	    (by
42	      intro Y Z f
43	      change (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map f) (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op a)
44	        = X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op a
45	      rw [← CategoryTheory.comp_apply, ← X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map_comp]
46	      congr 1)

48	/-- The scalar endomorphism of `O_X` given by a global section `a ∈ Γ(X, ⊤)`:
49	multiplication by `a`. Project-local helper. -/
50	noncomputable def scalarEnd (a : Γ(X, ⊤)) :
51	    SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf ⟶ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf :=
52	  (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf).unitHomEquiv.symm (globalUnitSection a)
53	
54	/-- `scalarEnd 1` is the identity endomorphism of `O_X`. Project-local helper for
55	identifying the diagonal entries of the chart quotient. -/
56	lemma scalarEnd_one : scalarEnd (1 : Γ(X, ⊤)) = 𝟙 (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) := by
57	  rw [scalarEnd, Equiv.symm_apply_eq]
58	  ext Y
59	  change X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op (1 : Γ(X, ⊤))
60	      = (SheafOfModules.Hom.val (𝟙 (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf))).app Y
61	          (1 : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj Y)
62	  rw [SheafOfModules.id_val, PresheafOfModules.id_app]
63	  exact map_one _

69	  ext Y

99	    scalarEnd a ≫ scalarEnd b = scalarEnd (a * b) := by
100	  apply (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf).unitHomEquiv.injective
101	  change SheafOfModules.sectionsMap (scalarEnd b)

105	  refine PresheafOfModules.sections_ext _ _ (fun Y => ?_)
106	  change (scalarEnd b).val.app Y (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op a)
107	      = X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op (a * b)
108	  exact (RingHom.map_mul (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op).hom a b).symm
109	
110	/-- `scalarEnd` is additive: `scalarEnd (a + b) = scalarEnd a + scalarEnd b`.
111	Project-local; underlies `matrixEnd` matrix-multiplication identity. -/
112	lemma scalarEnd_add (a b : Γ(X, ⊤)) :
113	    scalarEnd (a + b) = scalarEnd a + scalarEnd b := by
114	  conv_lhs => rw [scalarEnd]
115	  rw [Equiv.symm_apply_eq]
116	  refine PresheafOfModules.sections_ext _ _ (fun Y => ?_)
117	  change X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op (a + b)
118	      = (scalarEnd a).val.app Y (1 : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj Y)
119	        + (scalarEnd b).val.app Y (1 : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj Y)
120	  rw [scalarEnd_val_app_one, scalarEnd_val_app_one]
121	  exact RingHom.map_add (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op).hom a b
122	
123	/-- `scalarEnd` of a finite sum is the sum of the `scalarEnd`s. Project-local. -/
124	lemma scalarEnd_sum {ι : Type*} (s : Finset ι) (f : ι → Γ(X, ⊤)) :
125	    scalarEnd (∑ i ∈ s, f i) = ∑ i ∈ s, scalarEnd (f i) := by
126	  classical
127	  induction s using Finset.induction with
128	  | empty => simp [scalarEnd_zero]
129	  | insert a s ha ih => rw [Finset.sum_insert ha, Finset.sum_insert ha, scalarEnd_add, ih]
130	
131	/-! ## Matrix automorphisms of the free sheaf
132	
133	To realise the `GL_d` bundle transitions we promote an (invertible) `d × d` matrix of
134	global sections to an automorphism of the free rank-`d` sheaf `O_S^d`, exactly as
135	`chartQuotientMap` realises the universal matrix. The two key algebraic facts — that
136	`matrixEnd` turns matrix multiplication into composition and the identity matrix into the
137	identity — follow from the `scalarEnd` ring-hom identities above. -/
138	
139	/-- `SheafOfModules` over `O_S` has finite biproducts (it has finite products). -/
140	instance hasFiniteBiproducts_modules (S : Scheme.{0}) :
141	    HasFiniteBiproducts (SheafOfModules S.ringCatSheaf) :=
142	  HasFiniteBiproducts.of_hasFiniteProducts
143	
144	/-- A `d × d` matrix of global sections of `O_S` realised as an endomorphism of the free
145	rank-`d` sheaf `O_S^d`: the `(p,q)`-entry acts as `scalarEnd`, assembled over the rank-`d`
146	biproduct (mirrors `chartQuotientMap`). Project-local. -/
147	noncomputable def matrixEnd {S : Scheme.{0}} {d : ℕ} (M : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) Γ(S, ⊤)) :
148	    SheafOfModules.free (R := S.ringCatSheaf) (Fin d) ⟶
149	      SheafOfModules.free (R := S.ringCatSheaf) (Fin d) :=
150	  (biproduct.isoCoproduct (fun _ : Fin d => SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf)).symm.hom ≫
151	    biproduct.matrix (fun i p => scalarEnd (M p i)) ≫
152	    (biproduct.isoCoproduct (fun _ : Fin d => SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf)).hom
153	
154	/-- Composition of two `biproduct.matrix` morphisms is the matrix of pointwise sums of
155	composites — the categorical matrix product. Project-local helper for `matrixEnd_comp`. -/
156	private lemma biproduct_matrix_comp {S : Scheme.{0}} {d : ℕ}
157	    (mM mN : Fin d → Fin d →
158	      (SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf ⟶ SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf)) :
159	    biproduct.matrix (f := fun _ : Fin d => SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf)
160	        (g := fun _ : Fin d => SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf) mM ≫ biproduct.matrix mN
161	      = biproduct.matrix (fun i q => ∑ p, mM i p ≫ mN p q) := by
162	  refine biproduct.hom_ext' _ _ (fun i => biproduct.hom_ext _ _ (fun q => ?_))
163	  simp only [Category.assoc, biproduct.ι_matrix_assoc, biproduct.matrix_π, biproduct.lift_desc,
164	    biproduct.ι_matrix, biproduct.lift_π]
165	
166	/-- `matrixEnd` turns matrix multiplication into composition (with the order reversed by
167	the contravariance of the column/component indexing): `matrixEnd M ≫ matrixEnd N =
168	matrixEnd (N * M)`. Project-local. -/
169	lemma matrixEnd_comp {S : Scheme.{0}} {d : ℕ} (M N : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) Γ(S, ⊤)) :
170	    matrixEnd M ≫ matrixEnd N = matrixEnd (N * M) := by
171	  rw [matrixEnd, matrixEnd, matrixEnd]
172	  have hcomp : biproduct.matrix (fun i p => scalarEnd (M p i))
173	        ≫ biproduct.matrix (fun i p => scalarEnd (N p i))
174	      = biproduct.matrix (fun i p => scalarEnd ((N * M) p i)) := by
175	    rw [biproduct_matrix_comp]
176	    congr 1
177	    funext i q
178	    simp_rw [scalarEnd_comp]
179	    rw [← scalarEnd_sum, Matrix.mul_apply]
180	    exact congrArg scalarEnd (Finset.sum_congr rfl (fun p _ => mul_comm _ _))
181	  simp only [Category.assoc, Iso.symm_hom, Iso.hom_inv_id_assoc]
182	  rw [← Category.assoc (biproduct.matrix (fun i p => scalarEnd (M p i))), hcomp]
183	
184	/-- `matrixEnd` of the identity matrix is the identity. Project-local. -/
185	lemma matrixEnd_one {S : Scheme.{0}} {d : ℕ} :
186	    matrixEnd (1 : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) Γ(S, ⊤)) = 𝟙 _ := by
187	  rw [matrixEnd]
188	  have hmat : biproduct.matrix
189	        (fun i p => scalarEnd ((1 : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) Γ(S, ⊤)) p i))
190	      = 𝟙 (⨁ fun _ : Fin d => SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf) := by
191	    refine biproduct.hom_ext' _ _ (fun i => biproduct.hom_ext _ _ (fun p => ?_))
192	    simp only [Category.assoc, Category.id_comp, biproduct.ι_matrix_assoc, biproduct.lift_π]
193	    rw [Matrix.one_apply]
194	    by_cases h : p = i
195	    · subst h; rw [if_pos rfl, scalarEnd_one, biproduct.ι_π_self]
196	    · rw [if_neg h, scalarEnd_zero, biproduct.ι_π_ne _ (Ne.symm h)]
197	  rw [hmat, Category.id_comp, Iso.symm_hom, Iso.inv_hom_id]
198	
199	/-- An invertible `d × d` matrix of global sections induces an automorphism of the free
200	rank-`d` sheaf `O_S^d`. Project-local — the `GL_d` data underlying the bundle transitions. -/
201	noncomputable def matrixToFreeIso {S : Scheme.{0}} {d : ℕ}
202	    (M N : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) Γ(S, ⊤)) (hMN : M * N = 1) (hNM : N * M = 1) :
203	    SheafOfModules.free (R := S.ringCatSheaf) (Fin d) ≅
204	      SheafOfModules.free (R := S.ringCatSheaf) (Fin d) where
205	  hom := matrixEnd M
206	  inv := matrixEnd N
207	  hom_inv_id := by rw [matrixEnd_comp, hNM, matrixEnd_one]
208	  inv_hom_id := by rw [matrixEnd_comp, hMN, matrixEnd_one]
209	
210	@[simp] lemma matrixToFreeIso_hom {S : Scheme.{0}} {d : ℕ}
211	    (M N : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) Γ(S, ⊤)) (hMN : M * N = 1) (hNM : N * M = 1) :
212	    (matrixToFreeIso M N hMN hNM).hom = matrixEnd M := rfl
213	
214	/-- **Matrix automorphisms compose multiplicatively** (`lem:gr_matrixToFreeIso_mul`): the
215	forward maps of two matrix automorphisms compose to `matrixEnd` of the matrix product (with
216	the order reversed by the column/component contravariance). This is the linkage that turns
217	the matrix-level Cramer-inverse cocycle into a composition identity of sheaf-of-module
218	isomorphisms. Project-local. -/
219	lemma matrixToFreeIso_mul {S : Scheme.{0}} {d : ℕ}
220	    (A A' B B' : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) Γ(S, ⊤))
221	    (hAA' : A * A' = 1) (hA'A : A' * A = 1) (hBB' : B * B' = 1) (hB'B : B' * B = 1) :
222	    (matrixToFreeIso A A' hAA' hA'A).hom ≫ (matrixToFreeIso B B' hBB' hB'B).hom
223	      = matrixEnd (B * A) := by
224	  rw [matrixToFreeIso_hom, matrixToFreeIso_hom, matrixEnd_comp]
225	
226	/-! ## The tautological quotient on the charts -/
227	
228	/-- The **chart quotient** `u^I : O_{U^I}^r → O_{U^I}^d` (`def:gr_chart_quotient`):
229	left multiplication by the universal matrix `X^I` (`universalMatrix`). It is realised as
230	the morphism of free sheaves of modules whose matrix of components, in the standard bases
231	`(e_{i'})_{i' : Fin r}` and `(e_p)_{p : Fin d}`, is the universal matrix `X^I` injected
232	into the structure sheaf via `Scheme.ΓSpecIso`. Since the `I`-minor of `X^I` is the
233	identity, `u^I` is a split surjection onto the free rank-`d` sheaf.
234	
235	Project-local: Mathlib has no "matrix ↦ morphism of free sheaves" primitive. -/
236	noncomputable def chartQuotientMap (d r : ℕ) (I : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) :
237	    SheafOfModules.free (R := (affineChart d r I).ringCatSheaf) (Fin r) ⟶
238	      SheafOfModules.free (R := (affineChart d r I).ringCatSheaf) (Fin d) :=
239	  let A := CommRingCat.of (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
240	  let R := (affineChart d r I).ringCatSheaf
241	  haveI : HasFiniteBiproducts (SheafOfModules R) :=
242	    HasFiniteBiproducts.of_hasFiniteProducts
243	  let M : ∀ (_ : Fin r) (_ : Fin d), SheafOfModules.unit R ⟶ SheafOfModules.unit R :=
244	    fun i' p => scalarEnd ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso A).inv.hom ((universalMatrix d r I hI) p i'))
245	  (biproduct.isoCoproduct (fun _ : Fin r => SheafOfModules.unit R)).symm.hom ≫
246	    biproduct.matrix M ≫
247	    (biproduct.isoCoproduct (fun _ : Fin d => SheafOfModules.unit R)).hom
248	
249	/-- The chart quotient `u^I` sends the `(I.orderIsoOfFin k)`-th basis section of
250	`O_{U^I}^r` to the `k`-th basis section of `O_{U^I}^d`. Project-local: the column-`I`
251	restriction of `u^I` is the identity, the matrix-level content of `lem:gr_chartQuotientMap_epi`. -/
252	private lemma chartQuotientMap_ιFree (d r : ℕ) (I : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
253	    (k : Fin d) :
254	    SheafOfModules.ιFree (R := (affineChart d r I).ringCatSheaf)
255	        ((I.orderIsoOfFin hI k : Fin r)) ≫ chartQuotientMap d r I hI
256	      = SheafOfModules.ιFree k := by
257	  set A := CommRingCat.of (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ) with hA
258	  set S := AlgebraicGeometry.Spec A with hS
259	  haveI : HasFiniteBiproducts (SheafOfModules S.ringCatSheaf) :=
260	    HasFiniteBiproducts.of_hasFiniteProducts
261	  change SheafOfModules.ιFree (↑((I.orderIsoOfFin hI) k)) ≫
262	      ((biproduct.isoCoproduct
263	            (fun _ : Fin r => SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf)).symm.hom ≫
264	        biproduct.matrix (fun (i' : Fin r) (p : Fin d) => scalarEnd
265	          ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso A).inv.hom (universalMatrix d r I hI p i'))) ≫
266	        (biproduct.isoCoproduct
267	            (fun _ : Fin d => SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf)).hom)
268	      = SheafOfModules.ιFree k
269	  rw [Iso.symm_hom, SheafOfModules.ιFree, biproduct.isoCoproduct_inv]
270	  erw [Sigma.ι_desc_assoc]
271	  rw [biproduct.ι_matrix_assoc, biproduct.isoCoproduct_hom]
272	  have h1 : (CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.ΓSpecIso A).inv) (1 : A) = 1 := map_one _
273	  have h0 : (CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.ΓSpecIso A).inv) (0 : A) = 0 := map_zero _
274	  have hsub := universalMatrix_submatrix_self d r I hI
275	  have lift_eq :
276	      (biproduct.lift fun p : Fin d => scalarEnd
277	          ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso A).inv.hom (universalMatrix d r I hI p (↑((I.orderIsoOfFin hI) k)))))
278	        = biproduct.ι (fun _ : Fin d => SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf) k := by
279	    refine biproduct.hom_ext _ _ (fun p => ?_)
280	    rw [biproduct.lift_π]
281	    have hentry : universalMatrix d r I hI p (↑((I.orderIsoOfFin hI) k))
282	        = (1 : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) A) p k :=
283	      congrFun (congrFun hsub p) k
284	    rw [hentry, Matrix.one_apply]
285	    by_cases hpk : p = k
286	    · rw [if_pos hpk, h1, scalarEnd_one, hpk, biproduct.ι_π_self]
287	    · rw [if_neg hpk, h0, scalarEnd_zero, biproduct.ι_π_ne _ (Ne.symm hpk)]
288	  rw [lift_eq, biproduct.ι_desc]
289	  rfl
290	
291	/-- **The chart quotient is an epimorphism** (`lem:gr_chartQuotientMap_epi`): `u^I` is split
292	by the coordinate inclusion `s_I` of the `I`-indexed columns, hence is a (split) epimorphism
293	of sheaves of modules. -/
294	lemma chartQuotientMap_epi (d r : ℕ) (I : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) :
295	    Epi (chartQuotientMap d r I hI) := by
296	  have hsplit : SheafOfModules.freeMap (R := (affineChart d r I).ringCatSheaf)
297	        (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r)) ≫ chartQuotientMap d r I hI
298	      = 𝟙 (SheafOfModules.free (R := (affineChart d r I).ringCatSheaf) (Fin d)) := by
299	    refine Cofan.IsColimit.hom_ext (SheafOfModules.isColimitFreeCofan (Fin d)) _ _ (fun k => ?_)
300	    have key : SheafOfModules.ιFree (R := (affineChart d r I).ringCatSheaf) k ≫
301	          (SheafOfModules.freeMap (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r)) ≫
302	            chartQuotientMap d r I hI)
303	        = SheafOfModules.ιFree k :=
304	      (SheafOfModules.ιFree_freeMap_assoc (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r)) k
305	        (chartQuotientMap d r I hI)).trans (chartQuotientMap_ιFree d r I hI k)
306	    exact key.trans (Category.comp_id _).symm
307	  exact (IsSplitEpi.mk' ⟨_, hsplit⟩).epi
308	
309	end AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian

311	/-! ## Gluing a sheaf of modules along a scheme glue datum
312	
313	`Scheme.Modules.glue` descends a sheaf of modules from per-chart data plus a transition
314	cocycle over a `Scheme.GlueData`. Mathlib carries no turn-key module descent over a
315	scheme glue datum (confirmed), so this is an Archon-original construction. The
316	construction path (blueprint `def:scheme_modules_glue`): restrict to a chart through the
317	open-immersion pullback equivalence (`Scheme.Modules.overRestrictPullbackIso`) and glue
318	the local sections by locality of sections (`existsUnique_gluing'`).
319	
320	NOTE (scaffold): the body and the module-cocycle hypotheses on `g` are still to be filled;
321	the transition data `g` (per-overlap pullback isos) is recorded in the signature, the
322	multiplicative cocycle conditions remain to be added before the construction is closed. -/
323	
324	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules
325	
326	/-! ### Base-change transport of a transition isomorphism to a triple overlap
327	
328	To state the triple-overlap multiplicativity (C2) of a module descent datum we must
329	transport each transition isomorphism `g_ij`, living on the overlap `V_ij`, to the common
330	triple overlap `V_ijk = V_ij ×_{U_i} V_ik`. The transport pulls `g_ij` back along a
331	projection `p : V_ijk ⟶ V_ij` and reassociates the iterated pullbacks via the pseudofunctor
332	comparison `Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp`. The three scheme-level `glueData_bridge_*`
333	identities below (consequences of `t_fac`, `pullback.condition` and `cocycle`) line up the
334	endpoints of the three transports so that the cocycle equation is well typed. -/
335	
336	/-- **Base-change transport of a transition isomorphism along a morphism**
337	(`lem:modules_pullback_basechange_transport`). Given a transition isomorphism
338	`g : a^*Mᵢ ≅ b^*Mⱼ` over `V` and a morphism `p : W ⟶ V`, transport it to `W` as an
339	isomorphism `(p ≫ a)^*Mᵢ ≅ (p ≫ b)^*Mⱼ`, by pulling `g` back along `p` and reassociating
340	the iterated pullbacks through `Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp`.
341	
342	Project-local: this is the pullback-pseudofunctor packaging that lets the three transition
343	isomorphisms attached to a triple of charts be compared on a single triple overlap; Mathlib
344	has no descent-of-modules-over-a-scheme-glue-datum API. -/
345	noncomputable def pullbackBaseChangeTransport {W V : Scheme.{u}} (p : W ⟶ V)
346	    {Yi Yj : Scheme.{u}} (a : V ⟶ Yi) (b : V ⟶ Yj)
347	    {Mi : Yi.Modules} {Mj : Yj.Modules}
348	    (g : (Scheme.Modules.pullback a).obj Mi ≅ (Scheme.Modules.pullback b).obj Mj) :
349	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback (p ≫ a)).obj Mi ≅ (Scheme.Modules.pullback (p ≫ b)).obj Mj :=
350	  (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp p a).symm.app Mi ≪≫
351	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback p).mapIso g ≪≫
352	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp p b).app Mj
353	
354	/-- Triple-overlap bridge (source): on `V_ijk = V_ij ×_{U_i} V_ik` the two projections to
355	`V_ij` and `V_ik` followed by the overlap immersions `f_ij`, `f_ik` agree as morphisms to
356	`U_i`. This is the pullback condition; it identifies the sources of the `ij`- and
357	`ik`-transports. Project-local helper for the module cocycle (C2). -/
358	theorem glueData_bridge_src (D : Scheme.GlueData.{u}) (i j k : D.J) :
359	    pullback.fst (D.f i j) (D.f i k) ≫ D.f i j
360	      = pullback.snd (D.f i j) (D.f i k) ≫ D.f i k := pullback.condition
361	
362	/-- Triple-overlap bridge (middle): the `ij`-transition's target leg
363	`p^{ij} ≫ (t_ij ≫ f_ji)` to `U_j` coincides with the `jk`-transition's source leg
364	`(t'_ijk ≫ p^{jk}) ≫ f_jk`. Follows from `t_fac` and the pullback condition; it identifies
365	the target of the `ij`-transport with the source of the `jk`-transport. Project-local helper
366	for the module cocycle (C2). -/
367	theorem glueData_bridge_mid (D : Scheme.GlueData.{u}) (i j k : D.J) :
368	    pullback.fst (D.f i j) (D.f i k) ≫ (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i)
369	      = (D.t' i j k ≫ pullback.fst (D.f j k) (D.f j i)) ≫ D.f j k := by
370	  rw [Category.assoc, pullback.condition, ← Category.assoc, ← Category.assoc, D.t_fac i j k,
371	    Category.assoc]
372	
373	/-- Triple-overlap bridge (target): the `jk`-transition's target leg
374	`(t'_ijk ≫ p^{jk}) ≫ (t_jk ≫ f_kj)` to `U_k` coincides with the `ik`-transition's target
375	leg `p^{ik} ≫ (t_ik ≫ f_ki)`. This is the heart of the cocycle, derived from `t_fac`, the
376	pullback condition, `t_inv` and `cocycle`; it identifies the target of the composite
377	`jk`-after-`ij` transport with the target of the `ik`-transport. Project-local helper for
378	the module cocycle (C2). -/
379	theorem glueData_bridge_tgt (D : Scheme.GlueData.{u}) (i j k : D.J) :
380	    (D.t' i j k ≫ pullback.fst (D.f j k) (D.f j i)) ≫ (D.t j k ≫ D.f k j)
381	      = pullback.snd (D.f i j) (D.f i k) ≫ (D.t i k ≫ D.f k i) := by
382	  have key : pullback.fst (D.f k i) (D.f k j) ≫ D.f k i
383	      = D.t' k i j ≫ pullback.snd (D.f i j) (D.f i k) ≫ D.t i k ≫ D.f k i := by
384	    rw [D.t_fac_assoc k i j, ← Category.assoc (D.t k i) (D.t i k), D.t_inv, Category.id_comp]
385	  rw [Category.assoc, ← D.t_fac_assoc j k i,
386	    ← @pullback.condition _ _ _ _ _ (D.f k i) (D.f k j) _, key, D.cocycle_assoc i j k]
387	
388	/-- **Gluing a sheaf of modules along an open cover given by a scheme glue datum**
389	(`def:scheme_modules_glue`). From a glue datum `D`, per-chart sheaves of modules `M i`,
390	and transition isomorphisms `g i j` comparing the two charts' sheaves over the overlap
391	`V (i,j)` (after pullback), produces a glued sheaf of `O_{D.glued}`-modules.
392	
393	Project-local: Mathlib has no module descent over a scheme glue datum. -/
394	noncomputable def glue (D : Scheme.GlueData)
395	    (M : ∀ i, (D.U i).Modules)
396	    (g : ∀ i j, (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f i j)).obj (M i) ≅
397	        (Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i)).obj (M j))
398	    -- (C1) self-identity: over the diagonal overlap `V (i,i)` (where `t i i = 𝟙`) the
399	    -- transition isomorphism is the identity, i.e. the canonical isomorphism induced by
400	    -- `f i i = t i i ≫ f i i` (blueprint `def:scheme_modules_glue` (C1)).
401	    (_hC1 : ∀ i, g i i = eqToIso (congrArg (fun φ => (Scheme.Modules.pullback φ).obj (M i))
402	        (show D.f i i = D.t i i ≫ D.f i i by rw [D.t_id i, Category.id_comp])))
403	    -- (C2) triple-overlap multiplicativity: over each triple overlap
404	    -- `V_ijk = V_ij ×_{U_i} V_ik` the base-change transports
405	    -- (`pullbackBaseChangeTransport`) of the three transition isomorphisms `g_ij`, `g_jk`,
406	    -- `g_ik` satisfy `ĝ_jk ∘ ĝ_ij = ĝ_ik`. The three `glueData_bridge_*` identities, applied
407	    -- through `pullbackCongr`, line up the endpoints so the equation is well typed
408	    -- (blueprint `def:scheme_modules_glue` (C2), `lem:modules_pullback_basechange_transport`).
409	    (_hC2 : ∀ i j k,
410	        pullbackBaseChangeTransport (pullback.fst (D.f i j) (D.f i k))
411	            (D.f i j) (D.t i j ≫ D.f j i) (g i j) ≪≫
412	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (glueData_bridge_mid D i j k)).app (M j) ≪≫
413	          pullbackBaseChangeTransport (D.t' i j k ≫ pullback.fst (D.f j k) (D.f j i))
414	            (D.f j k) (D.t j k ≫ D.f k j) (g j k) ≪≫
415	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (glueData_bridge_tgt D i j k)).app (M k)
416	        = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (glueData_bridge_src D i j k)).app (M i) ≪≫
417	          pullbackBaseChangeTransport (pullback.snd (D.f i j) (D.f i k))
418	            (D.f i k) (D.t i k ≫ D.f k i) (g i k)) :
419	    D.glued.Modules :=
420	  -- **Effective descent as an equalizer of pushforwards.** The glued sheaf is the
421	  -- equalizer of the two canonical maps `∏ᵢ (ιᵢ)_* Mᵢ ⇉ ∏_{ij} (j_ij)_* (f_ij^* Mᵢ)`
422	  -- (`j_ij = f_ij ≫ ιᵢ : V_ij ↪ X`): the first map restricts the `i`-th chart section to
423	  -- `V_ij`, the second restricts the `j`-th and transports it across the transition `g_ij`,
424	  -- using the glue condition `(t_ij ≫ f_ji) ≫ ιⱼ = f_ij ≫ ιᵢ`. The cocycle hypotheses
425	  -- `_hC1`/`_hC2` are not needed to *construct* the object (they pin down the chart
426	  -- restriction isomorphisms `glueRestrictionIso`, built downstream). Pushforward preserves
427	  -- the sheaf condition and limits, so this equalizer of sheaves of modules is again a sheaf.
428	  let Qfun : D.J × D.J → D.glued.Modules := fun p =>
429	    (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.f p.1 p.2 ≫ D.ι p.1)).obj
430	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f p.1 p.2)).obj (M p.1))
431	  let P : D.glued.Modules := ∏ᶜ fun i => (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι i)).obj (M i)
432	  -- first leg: restrict the `p.1`-chart section to the overlap `V (p.1, p.2)`
433	  let aComp : ∀ p : D.J × D.J,
434	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι p.1)).obj (M p.1) ⟶ Qfun p := fun p =>
435	    (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι p.1)).map
436	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (D.f p.1 p.2)).unit.app (M p.1)) ≫
437	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp (D.f p.1 p.2) (D.ι p.1)).hom.app
438	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f p.1 p.2)).obj (M p.1))
439	  -- second leg: restrict the `p.2`-chart section, transport it across `g`, and reindex
440	  -- the immersion via the glue condition
441	  let bComp : ∀ p : D.J × D.J,
442	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι p.2)).obj (M p.2) ⟶ Qfun p := fun p =>
443	    (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (D.ι p.2)).map
444	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction
445	          (D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1)).unit.app (M p.2)) ≫
446	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp (D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1) (D.ι p.2)).hom.app
447	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1)).obj (M p.2)) ≫
448	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforward
449	        ((D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1) ≫ D.ι p.2)).map (g p.1 p.2).inv ≫
450	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardCongr
451	        (show (D.t p.1 p.2 ≫ D.f p.2 p.1) ≫ D.ι p.2 = D.f p.1 p.2 ≫ D.ι p.1 by
452	          rw [Category.assoc]; exact D.glue_condition p.1 p.2)).hom.app
453	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (D.f p.1 p.2)).obj (M p.1))
454	  let a : P ⟶ ∏ᶜ Qfun := Pi.lift fun p => Pi.π _ p.1 ≫ aComp p
455	  let b : P ⟶ ∏ᶜ Qfun := Pi.lift fun p => Pi.π _ p.2 ≫ bComp p
456	  equalizer a b

458	/-! ### Pullback of free sheaves along an arbitrary scheme morphism
459	
460	The functor of points (`AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.functor`) acts on morphisms by
461	pullback, and for that one needs `f^* (O^n) ≅ O^n` for an *arbitrary* scheme morphism
462	`f`. Mathlib's `SheafOfModules.pullbackObjFreeIso` supplies this only when the underlying
463	site functor `Opens.map f.base` is `Final`; the previous chapters discharged that `Final`
464	hypothesis only for open immersions and isomorphisms. The first lemma below removes the
465	restriction entirely: `Opens.map f.base` is `Final` for *every* scheme morphism, because
466	the structured-arrow category over any open `V` has a terminal object (the whole space
467	`⊤`). With that in hand, `pullbackFreeIso` and `pullback_isLocallyFreeOfRank` hold for all
468	morphisms. -/
469	
470	/-- For an arbitrary scheme morphism `φ`, the site functor `Opens.map φ.base` is `Final`:
471	over any open `V` of the target the structured-arrow category `{U : V ≤ φ⁻¹ U}` has the
472	terminal object `U = ⊤`, hence is connected. This is the missing ingredient that makes
473	`SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit`/`pullbackObjFreeIso` applicable to *every* morphism,
474	not just to open immersions. Project-local. -/
475	lemma opensMap_final {T' T : Scheme.{u}} (φ : T' ⟶ T) :
476	    (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map φ.base).Final := by
477	  constructor
478	  intro V
479	  set top : StructuredArrow V (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map φ.base) :=
480	    StructuredArrow.mk (Y := (⊤ : T.Opens)) (homOfLE le_top)
481	  haveI : Nonempty (StructuredArrow V (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map φ.base)) := ⟨top⟩
482	  apply zigzag_isConnected
483	  intro s t
484	  have hs : s ⟶ top := StructuredArrow.homMk (homOfLE le_top) (Subsingleton.elim _ _)
485	  have ht : t ⟶ top := StructuredArrow.homMk (homOfLE le_top) (Subsingleton.elim _ _)
486	  exact Relation.ReflTransGen.trans
487	    (Relation.ReflTransGen.single (Or.inl ⟨hs⟩))
488	    (Relation.ReflTransGen.single (Or.inr ⟨ht⟩))
489	
490	/-- **Pullback of a free sheaf of modules is free, for any scheme morphism**: for
491	`φ : T' ⟶ T` and an index type `I`, `φ^*(O_T^{⊕I}) ≅ O_{T'}^{⊕I}`. Built from
492	`SheafOfModules.pullbackObjFreeIso` once `opensMap_final` supplies the `Final` instance.
493	Project-local. -/
494	noncomputable def pullbackFreeIso {T' T : Scheme.{u}} (φ : T' ⟶ T) (I : Type u) :
495	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback φ).obj (SheafOfModules.free (R := T.ringCatSheaf) I)
496	      ≅ SheafOfModules.free (R := T'.ringCatSheaf) I := by
497	  haveI := opensMap_final φ
498	  exact SheafOfModules.pullbackObjFreeIso φ.toRingCatSheafHom I
499	
500	/-- The free-pullback comparison is natural in the base morphism: equal morphisms give
501	`pullbackFreeIso`s related by the `eqToHom` transport of their (differing) sources.
502	Project-local — used for the bundle-transition self-identity. -/
503	lemma pullbackFreeIso_eqToHom {T' T : Scheme.{u}} {φ ψ : T' ⟶ T} (h : φ = ψ) (I : Type u) :
504	    eqToHom (congrArg
505	        (fun α => (Scheme.Modules.pullback α).obj (SheafOfModules.free (R := T.ringCatSheaf) I)) h)
506	        ≫ (pullbackFreeIso ψ I).hom
507	      = (pullbackFreeIso φ I).hom := by
508	  subst h; simp
509	
510	/-- Iso-level free-pullback cancellation: for equal base morphisms `φ = ψ`, the composite
511	`pullbackFreeIso φ ≪≫ (pullbackFreeIso ψ).symm` is the `eqToIso` transport between the
512	(differing) pullback sources. Proved generically (`φ`, `ψ` variables, `subst`), so applying
513	it never forces the kernel to whnf a concrete immersion — the leaner replacement for the
514	`.hom`-level cast chain in `bundleTransition_self`. Project-local. -/
515	lemma pullbackFreeIso_trans_symm_eqToIso {T' T : Scheme.{u}} {φ ψ : T' ⟶ T} (h : φ = ψ)
516	    (I : Type u) :
517	    pullbackFreeIso φ I ≪≫ (pullbackFreeIso ψ I).symm
518	      = eqToIso (congrArg
519	          (fun α => (Scheme.Modules.pullback α).obj (SheafOfModules.free (R := T.ringCatSheaf) I))
520	          h) := by
521	  subst h; simp
522	
523	/-- **Pullback preserves rank-`d` local freeness.** If `M` is locally free of rank `d` on
524	`T`, then `φ^* M` is locally free of rank `d` on `T'`, for any scheme morphism `φ`. The
525	chart cover `{U i}` of `T` trivialising `M` pulls back to the cover `{φ⁻¹ U i}` of `T'`;
526	on each member the restriction of `φ^* M` is identified with the pulled-back chart-free
527	sheaf via the pseudofunctor comparison `pullbackComp`, the factorisation
528	`φ ∘ (φ⁻¹ U i).ι = (φ ∣_ U i) ≫ (U i).ι` (`morphismRestrict_ι`), and `pullbackFreeIso`.
529	Project-local. -/
530	lemma pullback_isLocallyFreeOfRank {T' T : Scheme.{u}} (φ : T' ⟶ T) {M : T.Modules}
531	    {d : ℕ} (h : SheafOfModules.IsLocallyFreeOfRank M d) :
532	    SheafOfModules.IsLocallyFreeOfRank ((Scheme.Modules.pullback φ).obj M) d := by
533	  obtain ⟨ι, U, hcover, hloc⟩ := h
534	  refine ⟨ι, fun i => φ ⁻¹ᵁ (U i), Scheme.Hom.iSup_preimage_eq_top φ hcover, ?_⟩
535	  intro i
536	  obtain ⟨e⟩ := hloc i
537	  exact ⟨(Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (φ ⁻¹ᵁ (U i)).ι φ).app M ≪≫
538	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (morphismRestrict_ι φ (U i)).symm).app M ≪≫
539	    ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (φ ∣_ (U i)) (U i).ι).app M).symm ≪≫
540	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback (φ ∣_ (U i))).mapIso e ≪≫
541	    pullbackFreeIso (φ ∣_ (U i)) (ULift.{u} (Fin d))⟩
542	
543	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules
